sanskrit:
क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते।
क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप।।
transliteration:
klaibyaṃ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayy upapadyate |
kṣudraṃ hṛdaya-daurbalyaṃ tyaktvottiṣṭha parantapa ||
literal_meaning: Do not go to impotence, O son-of-Pṛthā; this does not befit you. Abandon this petty heart-weakness and rise up, O scorcher-of-foes.
contextual_meaning: Krṣṇa confronts Arjuna’s sudden collapse of will-power, reframing it not as noble compassion but as a covert self-betrayal and identity diffusion.
psychological_state: Acute performance anxiety masquerading as moral sensitivity; ego-dystonic paralysis where the persona of the noble warrior is hijacked by the shadow fear of harming loved ones.
trigger_scenario: A star surgeon about to operate on a parent freezes in the OR, suddenly ‘unable’ to cut, rationalizing it as ‘first do no harm’ while unconsciously dreading the responsibility of life-or-death power.
anchor_text: Stand up and fight; weakness is unworthy of your true self.
